npcs.name: Abigail
npcs.description: Abigail is an adventurous young woman who loves exploring the mines and playing video games. She has a rebellious streak and an interest in the occult.

npcs.name: Alex
npcs.description: Alex is an athletic young man with dreams of becoming a professional gridball player. He can seem arrogant at first but has a soft side he rarely shows.

loved.name: Jack Be Nimble, Jack Be Thick

npcs.name: Elliott
npcs.description: Elliott is a romantic novelist who lives alone in a small cabin on the beach. He is eloquent and refined, with a love of literature and the sea.

npcs.name: Emily
npcs.description: Emily is a free spirit who works at the Saloon. She loves sewing, gemstones, and the desert. She has a mysterious and mystical side.

npcs.name: Harvey
npcs.description: Harvey is the town doctor, a gentle and caring man who worries about the health of the community. He has a passion for model aeroplanes and radio.

npcs.name: Haley
npcs.description: Haley is Emily's younger sister. She comes across as vain at first, but reveals genuine warmth and depth as you get to know her.

npcs.name: Leah
npcs.description: Leah is an artist living alone in a cottage at the edge of the forest. She loves nature, sculpting, and living simply.

npcs.name: Maru
npcs.description: Maru is Robin and Demetrius's daughter. She is intelligent and ambitious, with a passion for science and inventing gadgets.

npcs.name: Penny
npcs.description: Penny is kind and studious, tutoring the town's children in her spare time. She has a difficult home life but maintains a cheerful disposition.

npcs.name: Sam
npcs.description: Sam is an energetic skateboarder and aspiring musician. He has a fun-loving personality and is devoted to his family.

npcs.name: Sebastian
npcs.description: Sebastian is a quiet, introverted programmer who lives in his parents' basement. He feels misunderstood and craves independence.

npcs.name: Shane
npcs.description: Shane works at JojaMart and lives at Marnie's Ranch. He can seem unfriendly at first, but has a kind heart hidden beneath a rough exterior.

npcs.name: Pierre
npcs.description: Pierre runs the town's general store and sells seeds each season. He is Caroline's husband and Abigail's father.

npcs.name: Linus
npcs.description: Linus lives in a tent on the mountain by choice, preferring nature to society. He is wise, gentle, and deeply misunderstood by most townspeople.

npcs.name: Willy
npcs.description: Willy is the local fisherman who runs the fish shop on the beach. He has a weathered, salt-of-the-earth personality and a deep love of the sea.

npcs.name: Marnie
npcs.description: Marnie runs the animal ranch south of the farm. She sells livestock and animal supplies, and has a warm, motherly personality.

npcs.name: Clint
npcs.description: Clint is the town blacksmith, skilled at upgrading tools and cracking geodes. He is shy and socially awkward, particularly around Emily.

npcs.name: Robin
npcs.description: Robin is the town carpenter who can build and upgrade farm buildings. She is hardworking, cheerful, and takes pride in her craft.

npcs.name: Lewis
npcs.description: Lewis is the long-serving mayor of Pelican Town. He keeps the community together and has a secretive fondness for rare vegetables.

npcs.name: Caroline
npcs.description: Caroline is Pierre's wife and Abigail's mother. She tends a hidden sunroom full of tea bushes and enjoys a quiet, community-minded life.

npcs.name: Jodi
npcs.description: Jodi is Sam and Vincent's mother, managing the household while her husband Kent is away. She's warm-hearted and always trying to keep things together.

npcs.name: Kent
npcs.description: Kent is Sam and Vincent's father, a veteran who returns from war at the start of Year 2. He's adjusting to civilian life and carrying the emotional weight of his service.

npcs.name: Vincent
npcs.description: Vincent is Sam's younger brother and Jas's best friend. He's a bubbly, imaginative child who loves sweets and playing outdoors.

npcs.name: Evelyn
npcs.description: Evelyn is George's wife and Alex's grandmother. She's a warm, grandmotherly woman who loves baking, gardening, and looking out for everyone around her.

npcs.name: George
npcs.description: George is Evelyn's husband and Alex's grandfather. Confined to a wheelchair, he comes across as grumpy, but has a warm heart beneath his crusty exterior.

npcs.name: Gus
npcs.description: Gus is the friendly owner of the Stardrop Saloon, always happy to pour a drink or cook a meal. He's generous and the social heart of Pelican Town.

npcs.name: Pam
npcs.description: Pam is Penny's mother and the bus driver to the Calico Desert. She's rough around the edges and fond of beer, but genuinely cares about her daughter.

npcs.name: Sandy
npcs.description: Sandy runs the Oasis shop in the Calico Desert, selling rare seeds and goods. She's cheerful and friendly, longing for more visitors in her remote location.

npcs.name: Demetrius
npcs.description: Demetrius is Robin's husband, Maru's father, and Sebastian's stepfather. A dedicated scientist who studies the local ecosystem and can modify your farm cave.

npcs.name: Krobus
npcs.description: Krobus is a shadow creature who lives in the sewers beneath Pelican Town. Despite his appearance he's gentle and sells rare goods — and can become a housemate if you're single.

npcs.name: Wizard
npcs.description: The Wizard, M. Rasmodius, is a mysterious sorcerer living in a tower in Cindersap Forest. He's knowledgeable about the arcane and has a complicated history with the witch.

npcs.name: Dwarf
npcs.description: The Dwarf is an ancient being who lives in the mines and sells adventuring supplies. You need a Steel Pickaxe to break through to their shop, and the Dwarvish Translation Guide to communicate.

npcs.name: Leo
npcs.description: Leo is a young boy who grew up alone on Ginger Island, raised by parrots. After befriending him and unlocking the Island Farmhouse, he eventually moves to a treehouse on the Mountain.

items.name: Rusty Spoon

items.name: Ancient Seed

quests.name: Introductions
quests.description: The Mayor has asked you to introduce yourself to all the residents of Pelican Town. Getting to know your neighbours is the first step to building a thriving community.

steps.text: Meet Pierre at Pierre's General Store (9am–5pm).
Meet Caroline at Pierre's General Store or around town.
Meet Abigail — she's often at home or near the graveyard in the afternoon.
Meet Haley and Emily at 2 Willow Lane.
Meet Alex, Evelyn, and George at 1 Jodi's Court.
Meet Penny at the Library or Pam's Trailer.
Meet Sam at 1 Willow Lane.
Meet Harvey at Harvey's Clinic.
Meet Maru and Sebastian at Robin's House on the Mountain.
Meet Linus at his tent on the Mountain path.
Meet Robin at the Carpenter Shop.
Meet Leah at her cottage in Cindersap Forest.
Meet Marnie and Shane at Marnie's Ranch.
Meet Clint at the Blacksmith.
Meet Willy at the Fish Shop (Beach).
Meet Elliott at his Cabin on the Beach.

quests.name: How To Win Friends
quests.description: Pierre mentioned that gifts are a great way to build friendships with the villagers. Try bringing a gift to one of your neighbours.

steps.text: Check the Characters page to find loved gifts for the villager you want to befriend.
steps.tip: Loved gifts give 80 friendship points — 8x more than a neutral gift.

steps.text: Collect or buy a gift (flowers, crops, and minerals are good starting options).
Talk to the villager and press the gift button to give it to them. You can give 2 gifts per week, plus one on their birthday for triple the effect.
steps.tip: Check the Schedule Viewer to find where villagers are at any given time!

quests.name: The Skull Key
quests.description: You found the Skull Key in the Mines at level 120. There's said to be a skull-shaped cavern somewhere in the Calico Desert — this must unlock it.

steps.text: Reach level 120 of the Mines to obtain the Skull Key.
steps.tip: The elevator resets every 5 floors. Stock up on food and bombs before going deep.

steps.text: Travel to the Calico Desert by taking the bus from Pam (repair the bus at the Community Center or Joja, unlocking it).
Enter the Skull Cavern in the north-west of the Desert and use the Skull Key.
steps.tip: Bring Spicy Eel or Coffee for the speed/luck buff. Iridium ore becomes common past floor 100.

steps.text: Descend as deep as possible for Iridium Ore, Prismatic Shards, and other rare items.
steps.tip: Use staircases (crafted from 99 Stone) to skip floors on bad luck days.

quests.name: Getting Started
quests.description: Your grandfather left you Parsnip Seeds to start your new life on the farm. Plant them, tend to them, and harvest your first crop.

steps.text: Till a patch of soil with your Hoe and plant the Parsnip Seeds from your inventory.
Water your seeds each day using the Watering Can. Rain counts as watering.
steps.tip: Seeds that go unwatered for a full day don't die — they just don't grow that day.

steps.text: After 4 days the Parsnip will be fully grown. Click it to harvest.

quests.name: Forging Ahead
quests.description: Raw ore is useless until smelted. Craft a Furnace and put it to work turning your Copper Ore into metal bars.

steps.text: Mine Copper Ore from rocks in the upper Mines (levels 2–39).
steps.tip: The Mines open after the earthquake cutscene early in Spring. Bring a weapon.

steps.text: Craft a Furnace using the Crafting menu (25 Copper Ore + 25 Stone). Place it on your farm.
Load 5 Copper Ore + 1 Coal into the Furnace to smelt a Copper Bar.
steps.tip: Coal drops from rocks in the Mines, or can be bought from Clint's shop.

quests.name: Explore the Mine
quests.description: The Mines to the north-east have opened up after a recent earthquake. Head inside and see how deep you can go.

steps.text: Enter the Mines — head north-east of town, past the Adventurer's Guild.
Clear rocks and defeat monsters to find the ladder down. Reach level 5.
steps.tip: A ladder appears randomly when you clear rocks. Killing all monsters on a floor sometimes spawns one instantly.

quests.name: Deeper in the Mine
quests.description: The upper levels of the Mine are just the beginning. Push deeper — great riches and greater dangers await.

steps.text: Continue descending. The lift saves progress every 5 floors — use it to skip familiar territory.
steps.tip: Stock food before going deep. Upgrade your Pickaxe with Clint to clear floors faster.

steps.text: Reach level 40. The Ice Caverns begin here — enemies hit harder, but Iron Ore becomes common.

quests.name: Robin's Lost Axe
quests.description: Robin has sent you a letter — she lost her favourite Axe somewhere south of Marnie's Ranch and would love your help finding it.

steps.text: Head south from Marnie's Ranch into Cindersap Forest.
Search the south-east of Cindersap Forest near the river. The Axe is lying on the ground.
Return the Axe to Robin at the Carpenter Shop on the Mountain.

quests.name: Jodi's Request
quests.description: Jodi has written asking if you could bring her a Cauliflower — she needs one for a recipe she's been wanting to try.

steps.text: Buy Cauliflower Seeds from Pierre's General Store. They're only available in Spring.
steps.tip: Cauliflower takes 12 days to grow. Plant early in Spring to have time to complete the quest.

steps.text: Grow and harvest a Cauliflower, then deliver it to Jodi at 1 Willow Lane.

quests.name: Mayor's Purple Shorts
quests.description: Mayor Lewis has sent you a very private letter. He's lost something personal and is hoping you'll recover it — discreetly.

steps.text: Build 2 hearts of friendship with Marnie to unlock access to her bedroom.
Enter Marnie's bedroom (south-east corner of her ranch house) and pick up the Mayor's Purple Shorts.
steps.tip: The bedroom door is locked until you have the required friendship with Marnie.

steps.text: Return the shorts to Mayor Lewis. He'll be very grateful — and very embarrassed.

quests.name: Initiation
quests.description: Marlon at the Adventurer's Guild wants to see if you have what it takes. Prove yourself by slaying 10 Green Slimes.

steps.text: Visit the Adventurer's Guild east of the Mine entrance (open 2pm–10pm).
Kill 10 Green Slimes in Mine levels 1–39. They drop Slime and sometimes weapons.
steps.tip: Green Slimes are the most common enemy in the upper Mine. A basic Rusty Sword is enough.

steps.text: Return to Marlon to complete your initiation. You can now buy weapons, boots, and rings from the Guild shop.

quests.name: Linus's Berry Basket
quests.description: Linus has written asking about his blackberry basket — he lost it while foraging and can't remember exactly where he left it.

steps.text: Head to the path near the Backwoods, north of the farm. The basket is sitting near the bushes.
Return the basket to Linus at his tent on the Mountain path.

quests.name: A Winter Mystery
quests.description: On a crisp winter morning, you spotted a cloaked shadow figure near the Bus Stop. It fled the moment it saw you. What was it?

steps.text: Walk to the Bus Stop between 6am and 4pm during Winter. A shadow figure will appear and flee.
Follow the footprints south-west from the Bus Stop into Pelican Town.
Find the large bush near the playground in the south of town. Shake it — the figure is hiding inside.
steps.tip: Look for the bush near the path south of the Community Center.

steps.text: Receive the Magnifying Glass. Equip it to find Secret Notes hidden around the valley.

quests.name: Dark Talisman
quests.description: The Wizard has sent an urgent letter — his Magic Ink was stolen and taken to the Witch's Swamp. He needs the Dark Talisman from the sewers to break the seal on the passage.

steps.text: Obtain the Rusty Key by donating 60+ items to Gunther's Museum. This unlocks the Sewers entrance.
steps.tip: Geodes cracked open by Clint contain donatable minerals and artifacts.

steps.text: Enter the Sewers — use the grate south of the library in Pelican Town or the one in Cindersap Forest.
Talk to Krobus in the Sewers. He'll allow you to retrieve the Dark Talisman from the chest in the mutant bug lair.
Take the Talisman to the blocked cave at the Railroad and place it to unseal the Witch's Swamp.

quests.name: Goblin Problem
quests.description: A goblin is blocking the entrance to the Witch's Hut, where the Wizard's stolen Magic Ink is hidden. Find a way past it to retrieve the ink.

steps.text: Complete the Dark Talisman quest first to unlock the Witch's Swamp.
Obtain Void Mayonnaise — process a Void Egg in a Mayonnaise Machine, or buy from Krobus on Fridays.
steps.tip: Void Chickens hatch from Void Eggs placed in a Coop. Void Eggs can be bought from Krobus.

steps.text: Give the Void Mayonnaise to the goblin outside the Witch's Hut. He'll step aside.
Enter the Witch's Hut and collect the Magic Ink.
Return the Magic Ink to the Wizard. His tower basement is now open — it sells the Junimo Hut and other magical buildings.

quests.name: Cryptic Note
quests.description: A secret note found in the valley reads: "Hmm, I wonder if this brave soul can make it to the bottom of the Skull Cavern." Someone has been watching your progress.

steps.text: Find Secret Note #10. Secret Notes are discovered while foraging, mining, or fishing after obtaining the Magnifying Glass (A Winter Mystery quest).
Reach floor 100 of the Skull Cavern in the Calico Desert.
steps.tip: Bring Staircases (crafted from 99 Stone), Bombs, and high-energy food. Check the TV fortune teller — a lucky day makes a big difference.

steps.text: Return to the Casino entrance in the desert. Mr. Qi will be there to acknowledge your achievement.

quests.name: The Pirate's Wife
quests.description: An elderly woman named Birdie lives alone on Ginger Island's south beach. Her husband was a pirate who never returned from sea — she longs for a memento of him.

steps.text: Reach Ginger Island by repairing Willy's boat (1000g + 5 Iridium Bars + 200 Hardwood + 5 Battery Packs).
Find Birdie on the south beach of Ginger Island and speak with her.
Obtain a War Memento from Kent (available after he returns from war in Year 2) and give it to Birdie.
Follow the trade chain: Gourmet Tomato Salt → Stardew Valley Rose → Advanced TV Remote → Arctic Shard → Wriggling Worm → Pirate's Locket. Each item is obtained by trading with a different NPC.
steps.tip: The full trade chain spans NPCs across Ginger Island and the mainland. Check the wiki for each trader's exact location and requirements.

steps.text: Return the Pirate's Locket to Birdie to complete the quest.

quests.name: The Community Center
quests.description: Mayor Lewis mentions the old Community Center. When you investigate, you find a golden scroll in an unfamiliar language — and a group of small creatures called Junimos.

steps.text: Unlock the Community Center by entering it for the first time (no key required).
Inspect the golden scroll inside — you can't read the Junimo language.
The Wizard sends you a letter inviting you to his tower. Visit him in Cindersap Forest.
The Wizard grants you the ability to understand Junimo writing. Return to the Community Center.
steps.tip: The Wizard's Tower is in the south-west of Cindersap Forest. He's available every day.

steps.text: Read the golden scroll — the Junimos want you to restore the Community Center by completing bundles in each room.

quests.name: Restoring The Community Center
quests.reward: Completion of all Community Center Bundles
quests.description: The Junimos have asked you to restore the Community Center by completing all the bundles. Each bundle you complete repairs part of the building.

steps.text: Complete the Crafts Room bundles (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter, Construction, Exotic).
steps.tip: Forage items, resources, and exotic crops fill these bundles.

steps.text: Complete the Pantry bundles (Spring, Summer, Fall, Quality, Animal, Artisan).
steps.tip: Crops, animal products, and artisan goods.

steps.text: Complete the Fish Tank bundles (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter, Specialty, Crab Pot).
steps.tip: Various fish and shellfish from all seasons.

steps.text: Complete the Boiler Room bundles (Blacksmith, Geologist, Adventurer).
Complete the Bulletin Board bundles (Chef, Dye, Field Research, Fodder, Enchanter).
Complete the Vault bundles (2,500g, 5,000g, 10,000g, 25,000g).

quests.name: JojaMart Membership
quests.description: Instead of restoring the Community Center, purchase a JojaMart membership and fund community development projects directly.

steps.text: Purchase a JojaMart Membership from Morris for 5,000g.
Fund the Greenhouse project.
Fund the Bridge Repair.
Fund the Glittering Boulder Removal.
Fund the Minecarts.
Fund the Bus Repair.

quests.name: Journey To The Desert
quests.reward: Access to Calico Desert, Sandy's shop, and Skull Cavern
quests.description: The Calico Desert is accessible by bus from the Bus Stop, but the bus has been broken down for years. Repair it through the Community Center Vault bundles or the Joja route.

steps.text: Complete the Vault room bundles at the Community Center (or fund bus repair through the Joja route).
The bus is repaired! Pam drives it on workdays. Pay her 500g for a round trip.
Explore the Calico Desert: visit Sandy's Oasis shop, find the Skull Cavern entrance, and dig up artifacts.

quests.name: Ginger Island
quests.description: Willy's old boat is rotting in his back room. Repair it to sail to Ginger Island, a tropical paradise filled with resources, puzzles, and secrets.

steps.text: After reaching 5 friendship hearts with Willy, he shows you his back room with a broken boat.
Repair the Boat Hull: donate 200 Hardwood to Willy.
Repair the Boat Anchor: donate 5 Iridium Bars.
Repair the Ticket Machine: donate 5 Battery Packs.
Purchase a boat ticket from Willy for 1,000g and sail to Ginger Island.
steps.tip: The boat departs from Willy's shop. You can travel back for free once per day.

quests.name: Perfection
quests.reward: Perfection Rewards (Golden Scythe variants, farmhouse upgrades)
quests.description: Reach 100% Perfection by completing every major goal in the game. The Statue of True Perfection awaits.

steps.text: Ship every item in the game.
Cook every recipe.
Craft every item.
Catch every fish.
Complete the Museum collection (donate all artifacts and minerals).
Befriend every villager to maximum hearts.
Achieve Grandpa's 4-candle evaluation.
steps.tip: Have 12+ Grandpa Points by Spring 1, Year 3.

steps.text: Complete the Community Center or Joja route.
Achieve full Mastery (level 10+ in all 5 skills).

quests.name: Grandpa's Evaluation
quests.description: On the night of Spring 1, Year 3, Grandpa's ghost will evaluate your progress on the farm. Earn enough points for 4 candles to receive the Statue of Perfection.

steps.text: Earn at least 12 Grandpa Points before Spring 1, Year 3.
steps.tip: Points come from earning gold, friendship, and completing milestones.

steps.text: Receive the Statue of Perfection for achieving 4 candles.

quests.name: Marnie's Request
quests.description: Marnie has written to ask for an Amethyst. She'd like to give it to Jas as a gift.

steps.text: Obtain an Amethyst — found in the Mines on floors 1–39, inside Geodes, or as a rare forage item.
steps.tip: Geodes can be cracked open at Clint's Blacksmith for 25g each.

steps.text: Give the Amethyst to Marnie at her ranch south of town.

quests.name: Aquatic Overpopulation
quests.description: Demetrius needs data on the Carp population — the river is overrun. Catch 100 Carp within the 7-day window.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board outside Pierre's shop.
Catch 100 Carp from any freshwater spot: river, mountain lake, ponds, or the Secret Woods.
steps.tip: Carp are the easiest fish in the game — even a Bamboo Pole works. Add Bait to fish faster.

steps.text: Deliver all 100 Carp to Demetrius at the Science House before the deadline.

quests.name: Juicy Bugs Wanted!
quests.description: Willy is experimenting with a new bait formula and needs 500 pieces of Bug Meat within 7 days.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Kill Bugs, Cave Flies, and Grubs in the Mines — all three drop Bug Meat.
steps.tip: Floors 1–39 are packed with bug-type enemies. A good sword makes farming fast.

steps.text: Deliver 500 Bug Meat to Willy at his shop on the beach.

quests.name: Rock Rejuvenation
quests.description: Emily wants to attempt a dazzling gem fusion. Deliver one each of all seven standard gems within the time limit.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Gather all 7 gems: Amethyst, Topaz, Jade, Aquamarine, Ruby, Emerald, and Diamond.
steps.tip: Omni Geodes can contain any gem. Crack them open at Clint's Blacksmith for 25g each.

steps.text: Deliver all 7 gems to Emily at her house. She shares a home with Haley on Willow Lane.

quests.name: Gifts for George
quests.description: Evelyn wants to put together a seasonal gift basket for George. She needs a selection of crops from different seasons.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Gather the requested crops. Evelyn typically asks for spring staples (Leek, Cauliflower), summer fruit (Melon), and fall produce (Pumpkin).
steps.tip: Out of season? Check the Traveling Cart on Fridays and Sundays, or grow in the Greenhouse.

steps.text: Deliver the crops to Evelyn at 1 River Road.

quests.name: A Curious Substance
quests.description: Clint has found a mysterious residue inside Omni Geodes and wants samples for metallurgical research.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Collect the required number of Omni Geodes by mining and defeating enemies in the Mines.
steps.tip: Magma Sprites in the deep Mine (floors 80+) are a reliable Omni Geode source.

steps.text: Deliver the Omni Geodes to Clint at his Blacksmith shop on Robin's Road.

quests.name: Community Cleanup
quests.description: Linus wants to restore native plants to the forest floor. Gather 20 Mixed Seeds from weeds to help him replant.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Collect 20 Mixed Seeds by cutting weeds with your Scythe or any weapon.
steps.tip: Weeds are plentiful on your farm and around town, especially in spring and summer. They drop seeds when cut.

steps.text: Deliver 20 Mixed Seeds to Linus at his tent on the Mountain.

quests.name: Tropical Fish
quests.description: Gus wants to add an exotic dish to the Saloon menu and needs a supply of tropical ocean fish.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Catch 10 Tuna, 10 Red Snapper, and 10 Tilapia from ocean fishing spots.
steps.tip: Tuna: summer/winter. Red Snapper: summer/fall rainy. Tilapia: summer/fall. Fish the east pier for best access.

steps.text: Deliver the fish to Gus at the Stardrop Saloon.

quests.name: Crop Research
quests.description: Pierre is collecting crop samples for agronomic research. Deliver the requested seasonal produce within the 7-day window.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board. The exact crops required vary each time.
Grow or buy the requested crops. Common requests include Parsnip, Cauliflower, Melon, and Pumpkin.
steps.tip: The Traveling Cart (Fridays and Sundays) can supply out-of-season crops. Check your storage chest first.

steps.text: Deliver the crops to Pierre at his General Store.

quests.name: Island Ingredients
quests.description: Robin wants to study tropical farming techniques. She needs Ginger Island crops for research.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board. You'll need Ginger Island access first.
Sail to Ginger Island by repairing Willy's boat, then plant Taro Root and Pineapple crops there.
Harvest the crops and deliver them to Robin at her Carpenter Shop.

quests.name: Fragments of the Past
quests.description: Gunther is reconstructing an ancient skeleton from fossil fragments. He needs 20 Bone Fragments.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Collect 20 Bone Fragments from fishing in the Mines or digging at the Ginger Island Dig Site.
steps.tip: Fishing in underground Mine rivers and the Ginger Island fossil site are the most reliable sources.

steps.text: Deliver 20 Bone Fragments to Gunther at the Museum.

quests.name: Pierre's Missing Stocklist
quests.description: Caroline has posted a Special Order: Pierre lost his vegetable stocklist. She needs help recreating it by gathering produce samples.

steps.text: Accept the order from the Special Orders Board.
Gather the requested vegetables. Caroline typically asks for spring and summer staples.
Deliver the vegetables to Caroline at Pierre's shop.

quests.name: Let's Play A Game
quests.description: Mr. Qi challenges you to complete Journey of the Prairie King without dying — all three worlds.

steps.text: Unlock Qi's Walnut Room on Ginger Island by collecting 100 Golden Walnuts.
steps.tip: Walnuts are earned through farming, fishing, mining, and solving Ginger Island puzzles.

steps.text: Accept the challenge from Mr. Qi's Special Orders Board inside his Walnut Room.
Play Journey of the Prairie King at the Stardrop Saloon arcade machine and finish all three worlds without dying.
steps.tip: Eat food that doesn't give energy (like Coffee) before playing so your energy bar stays out of the way. Buy mid-game upgrades when offered.

quests.name: Extended Family
quests.description: Mr. Qi challenges you to catch all five Qi-Bean variants of the Legendary Fish using special Qi Bait.

steps.text: Accept the challenge from Qi's Special Orders Board.
Buy Qi Bait from Qi's shop (10 Qi Gems per pack). You must use Qi Bait at each Legendary Fish location.
steps.tip: Qi Bait lets you catch the Legendary II fish even if you caught the originals in a previous year.

steps.text: Catch all five: Ms. Angler (river north of JojaMart, Fall), Son of Crimsonfish (east pier, Summer), Radioactive Carp (Sewers), Glacier Fish Jr. (south island pond, Winter), Legend II (mountain lake, Spring).
steps.tip: Each is caught at the same location as its parent, but requires Qi Bait instead of the season/weather check.

quests.name: Danger In The Deep
quests.description: Mr. Qi activates Danger Mode in the regular Mine. Reach floor 100 while all monsters are harder and more numerous.

steps.text: Accept the challenge from Qi's Special Orders Board.
Enter the Mine. In Danger Mode all enemies have more HP and deal more damage. Descend to floor 100.
steps.tip: Bring your best sword, rings (Napalm Ring for clearing crowds), and stack HP-restoring food.

steps.text: Reach floor 100 to complete the challenge and claim your reward.

quests.name: Skull Cavern Invasion
quests.description: Mr. Qi activates Danger Mode in the Skull Cavern. Reach floor 100 under these harrowing conditions.

steps.text: Accept the challenge from Qi's Special Orders Board.
Travel to the Skull Cavern in the Calico Desert. All monsters are stronger in Danger Mode.
steps.tip: Pre-craft at least 100 Staircases (99 Stone each) and pack Bombs, Mega Bombs, and Lucky food.

steps.text: Reach floor 100 of the Skull Cavern in Danger Mode to complete the challenge.

quests.name: Four Precious Stones
quests.description: Mr. Qi demands four Prismatic Shards as proof you've mastered the Skull Cavern.

steps.text: Accept the challenge from Qi's Special Orders Board.
Farm Prismatic Shards from the Skull Cavern — they drop from Iridium Nodes and Omni Geode clusters on deep floors.
steps.tip: Each deep-dive run has a chance at 1–2 shards. Lucky days dramatically increase drop rates.

steps.text: Deliver 4 Prismatic Shards to complete the challenge.

farmTypes.name: Standard Farm
farmTypes.description: The classic balanced farm layout. Large open area with plenty of room for crops, animals, and buildings.
farmTypes.startingBonus: None — ideal for learning all aspects of the game.

farmTypes.name: Riverland Farm
farmTypes.description: A farm split into several islands connected by bridges. Great for fishing, tricky for layout.

farmTypes.name: Forest Farm
farmTypes.description: Forested farm with hardwood stumps that regrow monthly. Smaller crop area.
farmTypes.startingBonus: Unique foraging items spawn on the farm.

farmTypes.name: Hill-top Farm
farmTypes.description: Features a quarry with ore deposits. Good for miners.
farmTypes.startingBonus: Ore and gems spawn in the quarry.

farmTypes.name: Wilderness Farm
farmTypes.description: Monsters spawn at night on this farm. For a more challenging experience.

farmTypes.name: Beach Farm
farmTypes.description: A beach-side farm. Sprinklers don't work on the sandy soil! Great views.
farmTypes.startingBonus: Fish wash up on shore. Some plots cannot use sprinklers.

farmTypes.name: Meadowlands Farm
farmTypes.description: A lush farm introduced in 1.6. Starts with a pre-built Coop and two Chickens.
farmTypes.startingBonus: Starts with a Coop, 2 Chickens, and 50 Hay.
